golden_dataset_candidates.qualification_notes: AES-256-CBC with SecureRandom IV directly observed from EncryptionUtil.java. High confidence, accepted adjudication. Commit SHA pinned. evidence_refs non-empty. Full reasoning_trace. follow_up_suggestions present. Excellent training example for CBC mode without AEAD.
PBKDF2/SHA1/65536/256-bit directly observed from PBEKeySpec constructor. High confidence, accepted. Excellent KDF training example with concrete parameters.

BCrypt via DelegatingPasswordEncoder factory pattern. accepted_with_minor_edits (directness taxonomy correction applied). Good wrapper_usage / framework_default example for password hashing delegation.

golden_dataset_candidates.disqualification_reason: evidence_elevation_violation: JWT signing algorithm RSA/RS256 inferred from Spring AS framework default, NOT directly observed in source. ADJ-007 revised to needs_revision + escalate_to_human. Cannot qualify until explicit JwtEncoder @Bean or JWK Set endpoint fetched from source.
golden_dataset_candidates.qualification_notes: Requires: fetch build.gradle Spring AS version + check for explicit NimbusJwtEncoder @Bean. If RS256 confirmed from source: re-qualify at high confidence.
not_crypto confirmed — SpringSecurityPlatformSecurityContext: no crypto imports. accepted_with_minor_edits (evidence_refs normalization). Valuable negative example for security context classes.
Spring AS OAuth2 authorization server JWT issuance confirmed. accepted_with_minor_edits. Useful for training on OAuth2 server configuration analysis.
CRITICAL: TrustModifier trust-all SSL bypass. Accepted, all 6 checks passed. Highest-value security finding. Excellent training example for SSL misconfiguration detection.
FineractClient OkHttp insecure SSL bypass. Accepted, all 6 checks. SSLContext('SSL') deprecated string adds extra value. High training usefulness for SDK-level SSL bypass pattern.

OkHttp3Config production @Bean conditional trust-all. Accepted, all 6 checks. Most impactful SSL finding — affects all outbound HTTP calls. Flag-controlled bypass pattern is distinct from previous two.
not_crypto — domain acronym false positive. Accepted. Useful negative example.
not_crypto — business domain term. Accepted. Negative example.
not_crypto — PasswordValidationPolicy no crypto. Accepted. Useful example for password policy vs password crypto distinction.
not_crypto — Map key data structure false positive. Accepted. Easy negative example.
not_crypto — UUID idempotency key. accepted_with_minor_edits. Useful for 'key' lexical false positive training.
not_crypto — UUID external event idempotency. accepted_with_minor_edits. Same pattern as GDC-027 but different module.
not_crypto — S3ContentStoreService AWS SDK v2 plain ops. accepted_with_minor_edits. Useful for storage-without-client-side-crypto negative example.
not_crypto — BasicAuthRequestInterceptor Base64 only. accepted_with_minor_edits. Base64 vs crypto distinction training value.
v4 loop resolved. not_crypto — PlatformPasswordEncoder interface only. grep confirmed sole implementor DefaultPlatformPasswordEncoder. Good negative example: interface completeness check.

v4 loop resolved. crypto_finding — DatabasePasswordEncryptor wraps EncryptionUtil.encryptToBase64('AES/CBC/PKCS5Padding') + BCrypt.hashpw. Master password from FineractProperties (configurable). High training value: wrapper pattern + dual algorithm.

v4 loop resolved. crypto_finding — DatabasePasswordDecryptor wraps EncryptionUtil.decryptFromBase64('AES/CBC/PKCS5Padding'). IV prepend pattern confirmed consistent with HAR-005. Training value: symmetric decrypt wrapper, IV extraction pattern.
v4 loop resolved. not_crypto — UUID.randomUUID().toString() only, no javax.crypto imports. Negative example: UUID token generation vs cryptographic RNG distinction.

v4 loop resolved. crypto_finding — SecureRandom confirmed (not java.util.Random). nextDouble() bias minor security concern (should use nextInt(bound)). High training value: SecureRandom vs Random distinction + subtle bias issue.

v4 loop resolved. crypto_finding — JKS deprecated format, default keystore password 'openmf' in source, TLS version constraints commented out. Multiple certificate_issue concerns. High training value: multi-concern PKI/TLS config finding.

v4 loop resolved. crypto_finding — Liquibase CustomTaskChange invokes DatabasePasswordEncryptor.encrypt() → AES/CBC/PKCS5Padding 3-hop chain. Idempotent. No hardcoded key. High training value: deep delegation chain tracing in migration context.

v4 loop resolved. crypto_finding — TLS active but cipher/protocol restrictions commented out in application.properties. deprecated_protocol risk (TLS 1.0/1.1 not restricted). High training value: configuration omission as security finding.

v4 loop resolved. not_crypto — TwoFactorServiceImpl is orchestration only, no TOTP/HOTP library, no javax.crypto. Delegates to RandomOTPGenerator (HAR-009) and UUIDAccessTokenGenerationService (HAR-008). High training value: orchestration vs direct crypto distinction.

v4 loop resolved. crypto_finding — Liquibase XML uses Java customChange (TenantPasswordEncryptionTask), NOT SQL AES_ENCRYPT. AES/CBC chain confirmed. High training value: SQL ECB risk disambiguation, Liquibase customChange pattern.

v4 loop resolved. not_crypto — HashingService uses Guava Hashing.consistentHash() for bucket distribution, NOT java.security.MessageDigest. Agent 1 preliminary_imports was false positive. Highest training value: Guava Hashing vs cryptographic MessageDigest false positive correction.

v4 loop resolved. crypto_finding (test_code) — EncryptionUtilTest confirms AES/CBC/PKCS5Padding with SecureRandom IV (not static IV). Hardcoded masterPassword='fineract' is weak default dev password. Training value: test evidence confirming IV randomness + default credential security concern.

balance_assessment.positive_negative_ratio: 13:15 (slightly negative-heavy — acceptable for FP reduction training)

pqc_assessment.readiness_score_rationale: Score 1/10: No PQC libraries detected. Quantum-vulnerable RSA in JWT signing path (HAR-007/HAR-016). AES-256 symmetric encryption (HAR-001) provides acceptable quantum security margin (Grover halves effective key size: 256-bit → 128-bit effective — above NIST PQC threshold). PBKDF2/SHA1 (HAR-002) uses SHA-1 HMAC which has reduced security margin.

quantum_vulnerable_assets.asset: JWT signing — RSA (Spring AS default RS256)
quantum_vulnerable_assets.quantum_attack: Shor's algorithm — breaks RSA public key in polynomial time on quantum computer

quantum_safe_assets.note: AES-256 has 128-bit effective security against Grover — above NIST PQC threshold. Mode CBC is classical-safe but lacks integrity (AEAD recommended).

migration_recommendations: Replace Spring Authorization Server ephemeral RSA key pair with ML-DSA (CRYSTALS-Dilithium) when Spring AS supports PQC signing — target NIST FIPS 204
Upgrade PBKDF2WithHmacSHA1 to PBKDF2WithHmacSHA256 (short-term) and Argon2id (long-term) for key derivation — OWASP 2024 recommendation
Replace AES-CBC with AES-GCM (AEAD) for authenticated encryption — not a quantum issue but a security hygiene improvement
Increase PBKDF2 iteration count from 65536 to 600000 (OWASP 2024 minimum for PBKDF2-HMAC-SHA256)
Remove all trust-all TrustManager patterns (HAR-018, HAR-019, HAR-021) — these are pre-quantum security failures
pqc_assessment.threat_model_notes: Apache Fineract is a financial platform. The most critical quantum risk is the JWT signing path using RSA — if an attacker harvests JWT tokens today and later applies Shor's algorithm, historical authentication events could be replayed or forged. The SSL bypass vulnerabilities (HAR-018/019/021) are classical security failures that would also neutralize any PQC improvements by enabling MITM attacks.

cbom_completeness.exclusion_notes: HAR-016 excluded from CBOM as duplicate of HAR-007 (same Spring AS JWT finding). HAR-003 BCrypt parameterSetIdentifier null — work factor unverified, promoted with null parameter. All 9 crypto_findings have medium or high confidence. No low confidence exclusions.

Below OWASP 2024 recommendation of 600000 iterations
uncertainties: SHA-1 HMAC has reduced security margin vs SHA-256

analysis_task.objective: Confirm cryptographic operations in DatabasePasswordEncryptor

context_bundle.key_evidence: EncryptionUtil.encryptToBase64('AES/CBC/PKCS5Padding', masterPassword, data) + BCrypt.hashpw(password, BCrypt.gensalt())

input.instructions: Provide a cryptographic analysis for the provided evidence. Return structured findings, uncertainties and follow-up suggestions.

findings: crypto_finding — dual cryptographic operation: AES-256-CBC encryption via EncryptionUtil + BCrypt password hashing via BCrypt.hashpw.
masterPassword sourced from FineractProperties (configurable) — no hardcoded key.
Wrapper pattern: DatabasePasswordEncryptor delegates to EncryptionUtil for AES and directly calls BCrypt for hashing.
uncertainties: BCrypt work factor: gensalt() default is 10 — not explicitly set in this class.
follow_up_suggestions: Consider setting explicit BCrypt cost factor via BCrypt.gensalt(12) for stronger hashing.

analysis_task.objective: Confirm cryptographic operations in DatabasePasswordDecryptor

context_bundle.key_evidence: EncryptionUtil.decryptFromBase64('AES/CBC/PKCS5Padding', masterPassword, ciphertext) — IV prepended in ciphertext

input.instructions: Provide a cryptographic analysis for the provided evidence. Return structured findings, uncertainties and follow-up suggestions.

findings: crypto_finding — AES-256-CBC decryption via EncryptionUtil.decryptFromBase64.
IV prepend pattern confirmed: first 16 bytes = IV, next 16 bytes = salt, remainder = ciphertext.
Symmetric counterpart to DatabasePasswordEncryptor (HAR-005) — same key derivation chain.
uncertainties: masterPassword source confirmed as FineractProperties — production key management practice unverified.
follow_up_suggestions: Ensure masterPassword is injected from environment variable or secrets manager, not hardcoded.

analysis_task.objective: Determine if UUIDAccessTokenGenerationService uses cryptographic RNG for 2FA token generation

context_bundle.key_evidence: UUID.randomUUID().toString() — imports: java.util.UUID only. No SecureRandom, no javax.crypto.

input.instructions: Provide a cryptographic analysis for the provided evidence. Return structured findings, uncertainties and follow-up suggestions.

findings: not_crypto — UUID.randomUUID() uses platform-default RNG (typically SecureRandom on JVM), but UUID generation is not a cryptographic primitive.
No javax.crypto imports. Token generation is identifier-based, not cryptographic key material.
2FA access token is UUID-format — predictability risk lower than custom random but UUID v4 has limited bit entropy (122 random bits).
follow_up_suggestions: For high-security 2FA tokens, consider SecureRandom.nextBytes(32) base64-encoded for 256-bit entropy.

analysis_task.objective: Confirm RNG source and quality in RandomOTPGenerator

context_bundle.key_evidence: new SecureRandom() confirmed. OTP = (int)(secureRandom.nextDouble() * Math.pow(10, otpLength))

input.instructions: Provide a cryptographic analysis for the provided evidence. Return structured findings, uncertainties and follow-up suggestions.

findings: crypto_finding — SecureRandom confirmed (not java.util.Random). Cryptographically secure RNG is used for OTP generation.
Minor bias: nextDouble() * 10^N introduces modulo/floating-point bias. Not equivalent to uniform distribution.
OTP is numerical — typically 6 digits, effective entropy ~20 bits — adequate for short-lived OTP.
uncertainties: OTP length parameter source not verified — if caller passes small N, OTP space is very small.
follow_up_suggestions: Replace nextDouble() * pow(10, N) with secureRandom.nextInt((int)Math.pow(10, N)) for unbiased uniform OTP generation.

analysis_task.objective: Identify TLS configuration weaknesses in application.properties and keystore

context_bundle.key_evidence: server.ssl.enabled=true, server.ssl.key-store=classpath:keystore.jks, server.ssl.key-store-password=openmf, server.ssl.enabled-protocols commented out

input.instructions: Provide a cryptographic analysis for the provided evidence. Return structured findings, uncertainties and follow-up suggestions.

findings: crypto_finding — TLS active with JKS keystore. Three security concerns identified:
1. JKS format is deprecated since Java 9 — migrate to PKCS12.
2. Default keystore password 'openmf' hardcoded in source — critical credential exposure if repo is public.
3. server.ssl.enabled-protocols commented out — TLS 1.0/1.1 not explicitly restricted.
uncertainties: Whether production deployment overrides the default keystore password via environment variable.
follow_up_suggestions: Migrate keystore to PKCS12 format. Externalize keystore password to env var. Uncomment and set server.ssl.enabled-protocols=TLSv1.2,TLSv1.3.

analysis_task.objective: Trace encryption chain in Liquibase migration TenantPasswordEncryptionTask

context_bundle.key_evidence: CustomTaskChange.execute() → DatabasePasswordEncryptor.encrypt() → EncryptionUtil.encryptToBase64('AES/CBC/PKCS5Padding')

input.instructions: Provide a cryptographic analysis for the provided evidence. Return structured findings, uncertainties and follow-up suggestions.

findings: crypto_finding — 3-hop delegation chain confirmed: Liquibase customChange → TenantPasswordEncryptionTask → DatabasePasswordEncryptor → EncryptionUtil (AES-256-CBC).
Migration is idempotent — checks if already encrypted before re-encrypting.
No hardcoded encryption key — masterPassword from FineractProperties.
uncertainties: Key management in migration context: masterPassword must be available at Liquibase migration time.
follow_up_suggestions: Confirm masterPassword is available as environment variable during Liquibase migration execution.

analysis_task.objective: Identify TLS protocol version restriction gaps in Spring Boot security configuration

context_bundle.key_evidence: server.ssl.enabled=true, server.ssl.protocol=TLS — but server.ssl.enabled-protocols and server.ssl.ciphers are commented out

input.instructions: Provide a cryptographic analysis for the provided evidence. Return structured findings, uncertainties and follow-up suggestions.

findings: crypto_finding — TLS is active but protocol version and cipher suite restrictions are absent (commented out).
deprecated_protocol risk: Without explicit server.ssl.enabled-protocols=TLSv1.2,TLSv1.3, the JVM may accept TLS 1.0 and TLS 1.1 connections.
Configuration omission is a security finding — absence of restriction equals implicit permission.
uncertainties: JVM default TLS restrictions may exclude TLS 1.0/1.1 depending on Java version (Java 8u292+ disables TLS 1.0 by default).
follow_up_suggestions: Explicitly set server.ssl.enabled-protocols=TLSv1.2,TLSv1.3. Do not rely on JVM defaults for security-critical configuration.

analysis_task.objective: Determine if TwoFactorServiceImpl implements cryptographic OTP/TOTP algorithm

context_bundle.key_evidence: Delegates to RandomOTPGenerator.generateOTP() and UUIDAccessTokenGenerationService.generateAccessToken(). No javax.crypto, no TOTP/HOTP library import, no HMAC.

input.instructions: Provide a cryptographic analysis for the provided evidence. Return structured findings, uncertainties and follow-up suggestions.

findings: not_crypto — TwoFactorServiceImpl is an orchestration service. No cryptographic operation in this class itself.
OTP generation delegated to RandomOTPGenerator (SecureRandom — HAR-009). Token generation delegated to UUIDAccessTokenGenerationService (UUID — HAR-008).
No TOTP (RFC 6238) or HOTP (RFC 4226) library usage. No HMAC-SHA1/SHA256 for time-based token computation.
follow_up_suggestions: If TOTP compliance is required, replace custom OTP with a standard TOTP library (e.g., Google Authenticator compatible).

analysis_task.objective: Determine encryption mechanism in Liquibase changelog 0007_encrypt_existing_tenant_passwords.xml

context_bundle.key_evidence: changeSet id=2: <customChange class='...TenantPasswordEncryptionTask'/> — no SQL AES_ENCRYPT, no rawSql

input.instructions: Provide a cryptographic analysis for the provided evidence. Return structured findings, uncertainties and follow-up suggestions.

findings: crypto_finding — Liquibase migration uses Java customChange, NOT SQL AES_ENCRYPT.
SQL AES_ENCRYPT in MySQL uses 128-bit ECB mode by default — critical vulnerability if it were used. Confirmed absent.
Encryption path: Liquibase XML → TenantPasswordEncryptionTask → DatabasePasswordEncryptor → EncryptionUtil (AES-256-CBC).
follow_up_suggestions: Java customChange approach is correct. Avoid SQL-level AES_ENCRYPT functions in MySQL which use insecure ECB mode.

analysis_task.objective: Determine if HashingService uses cryptographic MessageDigest hashing

context_bundle.key_evidence: import com.google.common.hash.Hashing — single method: Hashing.consistentHash(long input, int buckets). NO java.security.MessageDigest.

input.instructions: Provide a cryptographic analysis for the provided evidence. Return structured findings, uncertainties and follow-up suggestions.

findings: not_crypto — HashingService uses Guava Hashing.consistentHash() which is a consistent hashing algorithm for bucket/shard distribution, NOT a cryptographic hash.
Agent 1 preliminary_imports incorrectly flagged java.security.MessageDigest — false positive. Actual import is com.google.common.hash.Hashing.
Guava consistent hashing has no cryptographic properties (no collision resistance, no preimage resistance). Purpose: load balancing.
follow_up_suggestions: No investigation required. Lexical false positive: 'Hashing' in class name does not imply cryptographic hash.

analysis_task.objective: Confirm AES IV randomness and default password strength via EncryptionUtilTest

context_bundle.key_evidence: testEncryptDecryptWorks(): two encrypt() calls produce different ciphertext — SecureRandom IV confirmed. masterPassword='fineract' hardcoded in test.

input.instructions: Provide a cryptographic analysis for the provided evidence. Return structured findings, uncertainties and follow-up suggestions.

findings: crypto_finding (test_code) — Test confirms AES/CBC/PKCS5Padding round-trip with SecureRandom IV. No static IV vulnerability.
Two consecutive encrypt() calls return different ciphertext — SecureRandom IV is properly randomized per call.
masterPassword='fineract' is hardcoded as default in test — same value used in dev application.properties. Weak default credential.
follow_up_suggestions: Ensure production masterPassword is overridden via environment variable and never uses the default 'fineract' value.

quality_fixes_applied: HAR-003: security_concern unknown → no_security_concern_identified; directness factory_or_provider_selection → framework_default (ADJ-003 taxonomy correction applied)
HAR-007: GDC-007 qualified → GDC-DISQ-007 disqualified (evidence_elevation_violation: RS256 inferred, not directly observed). ADJ-007 revised to needs_revision + escalate_to_human
HAR-018: security_concern deprecated_protocol → ssl_verification_bypass (correct taxonomy for trust-all TrustManager pattern)
HAR-019: security_concern deprecated_protocol → ssl_verification_bypass (correct taxonomy for trust-all OkHttp insecure mode)
HAR-027/028/030: directness unknown → direct_api_usage; cryptographic_operation not_applicable → none; evidence_refs confirmed present
not_crypto balance: 15 not_crypto / 13 crypto_finding — well-balanced for training
critical_security_findings: HAR-018: TrustModifier trust-all SSL bypass (ssl_verification_bypass) — production module
HAR-019: FineractClient OkHttp insecure SSL (ssl_verification_bypass) — SDK
HAR-021: OkHttp3Config production @Bean conditional trust-all — all outbound HTTP
HAR-010: keystore.jks default password 'openmf' + JKS deprecated + TLS version not restricted
HAR-012: TLS cipher/protocol restrictions commented out — deprecated_protocol risk
HAR-009: SecureRandom.nextDouble() bias in OTP generation (minor)
HAR-022: masterPassword='fineract' hardcoded as default in test and dev config
v4_loop_notable_findings: HAR-017: Agent 1 false positive — Guava Hashing.consistentHash() is NOT MessageDigest
HAR-013: TwoFactorServiceImpl has no TOTP/HOTP library — custom SecureRandom OTP only
HAR-014: Liquibase XML uses Java customChange — SQL AES_ENCRYPT (ECB) risk eliminated
HAR-008: UUID.randomUUID() for 2FA token — not_crypto, no SecureRandom misuse
pending_human_escalation: HAR-007: Fetch explicit JwtEncoder @Bean or JWK Set endpoint from Spring AS config to confirm RS256. Re-qualify GDC-DISQ-007 if confirmed.
